name: com.dungphan.memorytoolkit
description: Exemplary memory-handling utilities for Unity: object/GameObject pooling, collection and StringBuilder caches, a per-frame linear allocator over native memory, pool warm-up and low-memory trimming. Designed to minimize GC pressure and heap fragmentation.

samples.description: Projectiles from a warmed pool that release themselves — Instantiate/Destroy replaced by get/release.
A level that owns its memory: installer warms pools in a scene scope, everything is freed on scene unload, blocking cleanup only behind the load.
Round-based lifetimes shorter than the scene: unit pool, AI arena, and native score table owned by a match scope and freed in one Dispose.
Per-frame physics query with derived data in the frame arena — 0 B GC alloc per Update.
HUD text via StringBuilderCache, rebuilt only when values change.
Login scene loads configs and pins them to the Permanent scope; the scene's own memory stays momentary and dies on unload.
A game-side cache that sheds entries on MemoryManager.LowMemoryTrimmed alongside the automatic pool trim.
